subject: Geography
title: Delhi: Ancient and Exploding
description: A 60-second lesson on Delhi: Ancient and Exploding.

hook.text: Delhi has been destroyed and rebuilt at least seven times across 3,000 years of history.

buildup.text: Mughal emperors, British colonizers, and modern India all built their capitals here.

discovery.text: Greater Delhi is projected to surpass Tokyo as the world's largest city by 2030.

twist.text: Its air pollution regularly exceeds safe limits by 20 times, making breathing dangerous.

climax.text: Delhi is racing to modernize while choking on the pollution its growth creates.

punchline.text: The city that survived seven destructions now battles the air itself.

quiz.question: How many times has Delhi been destroyed and rebuilt?
